title: Share of people who identify as religious or spiritual
caption:
Share of people who identify as religious or spiritual

2023

This stacked bar chart displays the distribution of self-identified religious and spiritual affiliations across 24 countries, with horizontal bars summing to 100% for each nation. The data is segmented into five distinct categories: "Religious" (dark blue), "Both religious and spiritual" (light green), "Spiritual" (dark green), "Neither religious nor spiritual" (pink), and "Did not answer/'I don't know'" (grey). India shows the highest concentration in the "Religious" category, where the dark blue segment extends past the 60% mark, followed by Nigeria and Turkey, which also display large initial segments for this group. In contrast, Japan, Australia, and Sweden feature the smallest "Religious" segments, each occupying less than 10% of their respective bars. The "Neither religious nor spiritual" category dominates the right side of the chart for several nations; Spain, the United Kingdom, and the United States show pink segments that span roughly 40% to 50% of the total bar length. Germany and Hong Kong also exhibit substantial shares in this category, with the pink sections appearing larger than the blue "Religious" sections. The "Both religious and spiritual" and "Spiritual" categories vary significantly by region, with the Philippines and Tanzania showing prominent light green and dark green sections that collectively account for over half of their populations. Mexico and Brazil display a mix where the "Spiritual" dark green segment is notably wider than in European nations. The grey "Did not answer" segments remain minimal across all listed countries, appearing as thin slivers at the far right of each bar. The visual arrangement highlights a divergence where high identification as "Religious" in countries like India and Egypt coincides with low identification as "Neither religious nor spiritual," while the inverse pattern holds for nations like Spain and Japan.
